description: Client retry or duplicate submit created multiple resources or repeated side effects.
body: Failure mode: a mutating API has no idempotency key, uniqueness check, or duplicate outcome contract, so timeouts, refreshes, mobile retries, or webhook redelivery repeat side effects. Diagnostic trigger: implementation tests only the first success path and never repeats the same request. Do-not-use signal: do not describe a mutator as retry-safe without duplicate-path evidence. Recovery: add idempotency semantics, stable duplicate responses, or documented conflict errors; test repeated requests in safe local data. Risk boundary: downstream emails, payments, external webhooks, and background jobs may need their own dedupe evidence beyond the route handler.
